name: Jonathan Winters
bio: Jonathan Winters was an American comedian celebrated as a master of improvisation and character work, conjuring a gallery of voices and personas such as the sharp-tongued Maude Frickert. A formative influence on Robin Williams and generations of improvisers, he recorded acclaimed comedy albums beginning in 1960 and built a long parallel career in film and television.

timeline.ev: Records the classic album The Wonderful World of Jonathan Winters for Verve
Plays Lennie Pike in Its a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad World
Hosts his syndicated series The Wacky World of Jonathan Winters
Wins the Emmy for his role as Gunny Davis on Davis Rules
Wins the Grammy for Best Spoken Comedy Album for Crank(y) Calls
Receives the Mark Twain Prize for American Humor

accolades: Two Grammy Awards including Best Spoken Comedy Album (1996)
Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series (1991)
Mark Twain Prize for American Humor (1999)
Star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame (1960)
